area_description: Recommendation Systems with LLMs encompasses techniques that leverage large language models to enhance, replace, or augment traditional recommendation pipelines, spanning direct LLM-as-recommender approaches, LLM-enhanced feature engineering, conversational and knowledge-augmented recommendation, generative retrieval paradigms, and multimodal recommendation methods.

categories.name: LLM as Recommender
categories.description: Approaches that directly use LLMs as the core recommendation engine, leveraging their language understanding and generation capabilities to produce recommendations through prompting, in-context learning, or instruction tuning.

sub_topics.name: Prompt-based Recommendation
sub_topics.description: Using carefully designed prompts to cast recommendation tasks as natural language tasks, enabling zero-shot or few-shot recommendation without task-specific training.

sub_topics.name: In-Context Learning for Recommendation
sub_topics.description: Leveraging in-context learning to provide user-item interaction examples within the prompt, enabling LLMs to learn recommendation patterns from demonstrations at inference time.

sub_topics.name: Instruction-tuned Recommenders
sub_topics.description: Fine-tuning LLMs with recommendation-specific instructions to create specialized recommender models that follow natural language directives for various recommendation tasks.

categories.name: LLM-enhanced Features
categories.description: Methods that use LLMs to generate, enrich, or transform features for downstream recommendation models, including text embeddings for items, user profile construction, and review summarization.

sub_topics.name: Text Embeddings for Items
sub_topics.description: Using LLM-generated text embeddings to represent items with rich semantic features, improving item understanding and similarity computation in recommendation systems.

sub_topics.name: User Profile Generation
sub_topics.description: Leveraging LLMs to generate natural language user profiles from interaction histories, capturing user preferences, interests, and behavioral patterns in text form.

sub_topics.name: Review Summarization
sub_topics.description: Using LLMs to summarize and extract key information from user reviews, transforming unstructured feedback into structured features for recommendation enhancement.

categories.name: Conversational Recommendation
categories.description: Recommendation approaches that operate through natural language dialogue, enabling interactive preference elicitation, dynamic recommendation refinement, and natural language explanations of recommendations.

sub_topics.name: Dialogue-based Recommendation
sub_topics.description: Multi-turn conversational systems that recommend items through natural language dialogue, adapting recommendations based on ongoing conversation context.

sub_topics.name: Preference Elicitation
sub_topics.description: Techniques for actively extracting user preferences through strategic questioning and dialogue, using LLMs to ask clarifying questions and refine understanding of user needs.

sub_topics.name: Explanation Generation
sub_topics.description: Using LLMs to generate natural language explanations for why specific items are recommended, improving transparency and user trust in recommendation systems.

categories.name: Knowledge-augmented Recommendation
categories.description: Methods that incorporate external knowledge sources—including knowledge graphs, retrieved documents, and LLM world knowledge—to enhance recommendation quality, especially for cold-start and sparse-data scenarios.

sub_topics.name: KG-enhanced Recommendation
sub_topics.description: Integrating knowledge graphs with LLMs to provide structured relational knowledge about items, users, and their connections for improved recommendations.

sub_topics.name: Retrieval-augmented Recommendation
sub_topics.description: Combining retrieval mechanisms with LLMs to fetch relevant contextual information—such as similar user interactions, item descriptions, or domain knowledge—to augment recommendations.

sub_topics.name: World Knowledge for Cold Start
sub_topics.description: Leveraging the world knowledge encoded in LLMs to address the cold-start problem, providing reasonable recommendations for new users or items with limited interaction data.

categories.name: Generative Recommendation
categories.description: Paradigms that reformulate recommendation as a generative task, where LLMs directly generate item identifiers, textual descriptions, or sequential predictions rather than scoring from a fixed candidate set.

sub_topics.name: Generative Retrieval
sub_topics.description: Replacing traditional retrieve-and-rank pipelines with generative models that directly produce relevant items through autoregressive generation.

sub_topics.name: Item ID Generation
sub_topics.description: Methods that train LLMs to generate item identifiers (numeric IDs, semantic IDs, or tokenized representations) as output tokens, bridging language generation with item recommendation.

sub_topics.name: Sequential Recommendation with LLMs
sub_topics.description: Using LLMs for sequential recommendation by modeling user interaction sequences as text and generating next-item predictions through language modeling.

categories.name: Multimodal Recommendation
categories.description: Recommendation approaches that leverage multiple modalities—including text, images, video, and audio—using vision-language models and cross-modal alignment techniques to enrich item understanding and user preference modeling.

sub_topics.name: Vision-Language Recommendation
sub_topics.description: Using vision-language models to jointly understand visual and textual features of items for improved recommendation, particularly for visually-rich domains like fashion, food, and products.

sub_topics.name: Cross-modal Matching
sub_topics.description: Techniques for aligning representations across different modalities to enable cross-modal retrieval and recommendation, matching user preferences expressed in one modality with items described in another.

sub_topics.name: Multimedia Content Recommendation
sub_topics.description: Recommending multimedia content such as videos, music, and podcasts by leveraging LLMs to understand and match content across audio, visual, and textual modalities.

theme.name: Cold Start Problem
theme.description: Papers addressing the challenge of making recommendations for new users or new items with limited or no interaction history, leveraging LLMs' world knowledge and generalization capabilities.

theme.name: Fairness and Bias in Recommendations
theme.description: Papers examining and mitigating biases in LLM-based recommendation systems, including popularity bias, position bias, demographic bias, and fairness-aware recommendation strategies.

theme.name: Explainable Recommendations
theme.description: Papers focused on generating human-understandable explanations for recommendations, improving transparency, trust, and user satisfaction through interpretable recommendation reasoning.

theme.name: Analysis
theme.description: Papers where experiments are conducted to evaluate the performance of baseline solutions, showing gaps and future directions.

theme.name: Benchmark
theme.description: Papers that introduce new benchmark datasets and evaluation metrics.

theme.name: Application
theme.description: Papers that show how to apply the techniques to a specific domain or task, highlighting its strengths and gaps.

theme.name: Survey
theme.description: Papers that provide a comprehensive overview of the current state of the art, including its taxonomy, advantages and limitations.
